_comment: Simplified alliance / bloc graph for the educational escalation simulator. EDUCATIONAL MODEL ONLY — not a real geopolitical claim, not operational. Groupings are broad approximations to show how a major exchange would cascade across blocs. Sources: publicly available alliance treaties and diplomatic relationships as of 2023.

NATO.description: North Atlantic Treaty Organization — collective defence treaty (Article 5)

WesternBloc._comment: Broader US-led Western bloc including treaty allies in the Indo-Pacific. Non-nuclear allies still benefit from nuclear deterrence extended by the nuclear members. EDUCATIONAL SIMPLIFICATION.
WesternBloc.description: Western / US-led alliance bloc (NATO + Pacific treaty allies) — simplified educational model

EasternBloc._comment: Broader adversarial / Eastern bloc grouping. China and North Korea are treated as aligned here for escalation modelling purposes only. Iran included as a geopolitically aligned actor. EDUCATIONAL SIMPLIFICATION.
EasternBloc.description: Eastern / adversarial bloc (Russia + CSTO + China-NK axis) — simplified educational model

India-standalone.description: India maintains a policy of strategic autonomy and acts independently

Pakistan-standalone.description: Pakistan acts independently (not formally aligned to either bloc)
